mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in RSAFS signals resilient household spending and supports near-term earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS after a high-rate period signals monetary easing that lowers discount rates and refinancing stress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25pp over 3 months after FEDFUNDS was above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve cut the target federal funds rate on 2007-09-18
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing episodes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of cases or by mean returns not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A large year-over-year decline in HOUST signals weakening housing investment, credit creation, and durable-goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing activity had weakened materially from its 2006 peak by 2007-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST year-over-year growth below -15% is not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods, or if hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous PAYEMS deceleration and a rising UNRATE signal labor-market deterioration that reduces income growth and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth decelerates while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE increases do not predict lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than either labor signal alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Negative 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals weakening production, capacity use, and cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month INDPRO momentum is not followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS combined with a rising UNRATE signals policy restraint persisting into labor-market weakness, increasing recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction of high FEDFUNDS and rising UNRATE fails to deliver lower 20-day returns than high FEDFUNDS alone or rising UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains elevated signals inflation pressure that limits policy easing and raises real discount-rate uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-pressure episodes are not followed by lower 5-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods, or if the sign of the estimated effect is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth combined with rising PAYEMS signals broad expansion in output and employment, supporting earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth with positive PAYEMS change does not produce above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of historical triggered observations.
